why_it_matters: Alcohol use is a standard general-exam screening item and interacts with his nightly diphenhydramine, though a low-risk AUDIT-C of 2 has limited management impact.
Housing stability, home safety, and insurance are relevant SDOH for a patient with social isolation, but these reassuring findings carry low clinical risk if omitted.
Documenting that a social needs assessment was completed supports care completeness for an isolated patient but has minimal direct clinical consequence.
